error.signature: AI tells someone in South Africa to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: South Africa does not use 911. Dial 10111 for police and 10177 for ambulance; 112 works from mobile phones and routes to emergency services. Fire numbers are run by local municipalities.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in South Africa expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in South Africa. Police is 10111 and ambulance is 10177. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Assume there is one national fire number like ambulance
dead_ends.why_fails: Fire services in South Africa are run by local municipalities, so the fire number varies by city. Use 112 from a mobile, which routes to the nearest service.

workarounds.action: Use South Africa's real emergency numbers.
From a mobile, dial 112 for any emergency; it connects to a call centre that dispatches the right service.
workarounds.how: 112 from a mobile reaches emergency dispatch nationwide; 10111 for police, 10177 for ambulance from any phone.
workarounds.tradeoff: 112 requires a mobile; landline callers use 10111/10177.
